title: Basic Transaction with COMMIT
description: Demonstrates a simple transaction that transfers money between accounts with COMMIT
explanation: This transaction debits Rs.5000 from ACC001 and credits it to ACC002. COMMIT makes both changes permanent. If either UPDATE fails, neither change occurs (atomicity).

title: Transaction with SAVEPOINT for Partial Rollback
description: Shows how to create savepoints and rollback to specific points within a transaction
explanation: Creates a checkpoint after order insertion. If order items fail, we rollback to savepoint keeping the order but discarding items. Allows partial success handling.

title: Setting Transaction Isolation Level
description: SQL syntax for setting different isolation levels to control concurrency
explanation: SERIALIZABLE prevents all concurrency problems but may cause blocking. READ COMMITTED is the default in SQL Server/Oracle allowing better concurrency but with some risks.

title: Handling Concurrency with Explicit Locking
description: Demonstrates explicit locking to control concurrent access to data
code:
-- Using exclusive lock for update
BEGIN TRANSACTION;

SELECT * FROM Account
WHERE account_id = 'ACC001'
FOR UPDATE; -- Acquires exclusive lock

UPDATE Account
SET balance = balance - 1000
WHERE account_id = 'ACC001';

COMMIT; -- Lock released on commit

-- Shared lock example for reading
BEGIN TRANSACTION;

SELECT * FROM Account
WHERE account_id = 'ACC001'
FOR SHARE; -- Acquires shared lock (in PostgreSQL)

-- Other transactions can read but not modify

COMMIT;
explanation: FOR UPDATE acquires exclusive lock blocking all others. FOR SHARE allows concurrent reads. This explicit locking gives fine-grained control over concurrency.

title: Transaction with Error Handling
description: Demonstrates transaction with proper error handling using TRY-CATCH
explanation: TRY-CATCH provides structured error handling. If any error occurs, ROLLBACK executes automatically. This ensures atomicity even when errors happen mid-transaction.
